news.t: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Gain Ground
Oil and gas services ETFs power higher
Energy equipment ETFs outperform Wednesday, on track for best month on record
Oil prices edge up on stimulus support despite ample supplies
Energy ETFs fall as U.S. oil prices resume sell-off, plunge more than 25%
Energy ETFs Surge Amid Trade and Middle East News
These 5 Beaten Down ETFs May Be Oversold

holdings.infoBox: IEZ is an equity ETF with a total of 35 individual holdings. The top holdings are SLB N.V. stock at 23.28%, Baker Hughes Company at 22.11%, TechnipFMC at 4.44%, Halliburton Company at 4.44%, and NOV Inc. at 3.80%.

overview.description: The iShares U.S. Oil Equipment & Services ETF (IEZ) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in energy equity. The fund tracks a market-cap-weighted index of domestic companies that are suppliers of equipment or services to oil fields and offshore platforms. IEZ was launched on May 1, 2006 and is issued by BlackRock.
infoTable: DJ US Select / Oil Equipment & Services

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Gain Ground
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were higher, fluctuating from one day to the next as the market weighs fading heating demand against a gradual pickup in cooling needs.

data.title: Oil and gas services ETFs power higher
data.text: Energy services exchange-traded funds rallied Thursday, a rare sector in the black on a day of steep sell-offs. The VanEck Vectors Oil Services ETF gained 2.1% by mid-afternoon, the SPDR S&P Oil & Gas...

data.title: Energy equipment ETFs outperform Wednesday, on track for best month on record
data.text: Shares of oil and gas equipment and services exchange-traded funds surged Wednesday, as investors broadened their exposure to the beaten-down energy sector in anticipation of stronger economic activit...

data.title: Oil prices edge up on stimulus support despite ample supplies
data.text: Oil prices nudged up on Monday, with Brent futures set to post a fifth straight monthly gain, as global stimulus measures underpin prices even as demand struggles to return to pre-COVID levels in a we...

data.title: Energy ETFs fall as U.S. oil prices resume sell-off, plunge more than 25%
data.text: Exchange-traded funds with exposure to the oil sector remained under pressure Monday as oil [CLM20] looked to be on track for the second-worst decline in history based on the most-active contract.

data.title: Energy ETFs Surge Amid Trade and Middle East News
data.text: A partial trade agreement and ongoing tensions in the Middle East may fuel oil stocks this week. Gain exposure to the sector using these three ETFs.
